blockers.description: The prompt asks for the schools with the "best" students, which can be interpreted by SAT score in each of the three fields (or their average), NumGE1500
example_questions: What do you mean by the schools with the 'best' students?
When filtering for the schools with the "best" students, should I use their SAT score?
What should I use to filter by the schools with the "best" students?
blockers.resolution: When looking for the schools with the "best" student, use their average SAT score in math

blockers.description: The prompt asks for "northern schools", which makes it unclear which schools can be considered as such
example_questions: What latitude should I consider for "northern schools"?
Which are the latitude values for "northern schools"?
Which latitude values should I use to look "northern schools"?
blockers.resolution: The northern schools are those with a latitude over 37.7.

blockers.description: The prompt mentions the "legislative name", but it is not explained what this term means.
example_questions: What does the "legislative name" refer to?
What should I consider as the "legislative name"?
blockers.resolution:
The "legislative name" is obtained as: "{ZIP}: {State}, {school's name}". 
Only include schools where Zip, State, and School name are not NULL.

blockers.description: The "StatusType" column in the "schools" table contains values that cannot be interpreted. It is impossible to understand how to use this column to determine whether a school is active or not.
example_questions: How should I interpret the values from the "StatusType" column in the "schools" table?
What do the values from the "StatusType" column in the "schools" table mean?
How do I interpret the values from the "StatusType" column in the "schools" table?
blockers.resolution: A school is active if the "StatusType" column in the "schools" table contains the values "3", "J", "S", or "T".
